_readme: Add poems here. Each poem needs: id (unique number), title, content (full poem text with line breaks as \n), date (YYYY-MM-DD), tags (array of strings), excerpt (optional short preview). Save the file and push to GitHub — the site updates automatically for everyone.

poems.title: The Space Between Words
poems.excerpt: There are things I never said — they live in the margins of my letters, in the pauses of my sentences...
poems.content:
There are things I never said —
they live in the margins of my letters,
in the pauses of my sentences,
in the white space between words
that were never enough.

I have been writing you
for longer than I have known your name,
calling out in every poem
like a letter with no address.

Perhaps that is what poetry is:
the art of saying everything
by barely saying anything at all.

And perhaps that is what you are:
the silence that makes the music make sense.

poems.title: Boundary Ropes
poems.excerpt: The boundary rope is not an ending — it is a promise the field makes to the sky...
poems.content:
The boundary rope is not an ending —
it is a promise the field makes to the sky:
that if you hit it hard enough,
you can reach anywhere.

I have stood at the crease at dawn
when the dew still hangs like questions
and the pitch is as uncertain as my future.

But I have learned this:
you do not need to see where the ball will land.
You only need to trust your arms,
your breathing,
the years of silence that became muscle.

Every cover drive is a small prayer.
Every six is the universe answering.

poems.title: Sir HV's Classroom
poems.excerpt: He never taught from the board — he taught from the window, from the weather...
poems.content:
He never taught from the board —
he taught from the window,
from the weather,
from the way a sentence could hold a whole life
if you let it.

He said: literature is not a subject.
It is a way of surviving.

I did not understand then.
I was sixteen and restless
and the world outside was louder
than any poem.

But now I hear him in everything I write —
his voice in the margin, asking:
Is this true? Is this yours? Is this enough?

The answer is always: because of you,
yes.

poems.title: Delhi, October
poems.excerpt: The smog has not come yet. The trees on the campus road still believe in themselves...
poems.content:
The smog has not come yet.
The trees on the campus road still believe in themselves,
still wearing their green like an argument
against the city's grey intentions.

I walk here between lectures
with my notebook pressed to my chest
like armour, like a promise.

Delhi does not care about poetry.
Delhi runs on noise and ambition
and the smell of chai at six in the morning.

But I have found a way to love it anyway —
the way you love anything complicated:
by paying attention,
by staying.

poems.title: For Tom
poems.excerpt: Before I knew what to write, I knew I would write it for you...
poems.content:
Before I knew what to write,
I knew I would write it for you.

That is the strange thing about inspiration —
it does not arrive with instructions.
It arrives as a person,
standing in a doorway,
looking like they belong in every poem
you have not yet written.

You have been the subject
and the reason
and the reader I imagine
when the words refuse to come.

I do not know how to thank you for that.
So I keep writing.
That is the only language I have
that comes close to what I mean.
